perturbation.original_text: (b) Other Definitions (i) “Claim Information” means a Claim Request and/or any associated Claim Response. (ii) “Claim Request” means invoices (and may also include treatment plans) of a Provider User submitted to an Insurer User by or on behalf of a Provider User through the HCAI System. (iii) “Claim Response” means a response by an Insurer User to a Provider User that is submitted by such Insurer User to the HCAI System for transmission to the originating Provider User. (iv) “Claims” means any claims, demands, actions, causes of action, suits or proceedings under any law or theory of law (including negligence, product liability, or breach of contract whether or not a fundamental breach or breach of a fundamental term). (v) “Insurer User” means an Insurer that participates in the HCAI System. (vi) “Personal Information” means any information about an identifiable i ndividual as defined in the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (Canada) and include s personal health information as defined in the Personal Health Info rmation Protection Act, 2004 (Ontar io), and any amending or successor legislation to either statute and th e regulations made pursuant thereto. (vii) “Privacy Laws” means the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (Canada), the Personal Health Information Protection Act , 2004 (Ontario) and any other laws enforceable in Ontario, now existing or which may be enacted in the future, which govern the collection, use, processing and/or disclosure - 2 - PRACTICE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM INTEGRATION TOOLKIT NON-DISCLOSURE AND PERMITTED USE AGREEMENT of personal information, and any ame nding or successor legislation and regul ations made pursuant thereto, ,all as may be amended from time to time. (viii) “Provider User” or “User” means an individual Provider that participates as a “provider” in the HCAI System and is bound by the HCAI Electronic Access Terms and Conditions. (ix) “Services” means the services provided by IBC directly or through its subcontract ors in connection with the transmission and submission of Claim Requests from Provider Users to Insu rer Users using the HCAI System, and the transport of Claim Responses in respect of su ch claims, where provided in electronic form, back from Insurers to Providers, and other ancillar y activities as IBC may elect to provide.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Other Definitions (i) “Claim Information” means information related to a claim. (ii) “Claim Request” means a request related to a claim. (iii) “Claim Response” means a response to a claim. (iv) “Claims” means any demands or actions. (v) “Insurer User” means an Insurer. (vi) “Personal Information” means information about an individual. (vii) “Privacy Laws” means laws governing personal information. (viii) “Provider User” or “User” means an individual Provider. (ix) “Services” means services provided by IBC.
perturbation.explanation: The deletion of specific details from the 'Other Definitions' section, particularly concerning the HCAI System, Providers, Insurers, and electronic submissions, renders the remaining text vague and contradicts later sections that assume these specific definitions are in place. For example, the section referencing 'HCAI System authentication credentials' now lacks context.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (g) From time to time IBC may provide updates or changes to the Confidential Information. Upon receipt of any such update or change, Vendor shall promptly review such informati on and if necessary will use commercially reasonable efforts to issue an upgrade to its PMS System which will permit such PMS System to continue to communicate with the HCAI System.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes key specifics from the 'Other Definitions' section, such as the full definitions of 'Claim Request,' 'Claim Response,' 'Insurer User,' 'Provider User,' and most importantly, the 'HCAI System'. The contradicted text refers to the 'HCAI System', and the change makes it so that the document no longer defines that term specifically, causing a direct contradiction. This makes the continued reference to the 'HCAI System' in the contradicted section incompatible with the newly simplified definitions, as the Vendor will no longer understand what it means to communicate with the HCAI system.

perturbation.original_text: (f) To the extent any Confidential Info rmation contains any Personal Informa tion, Vendor agrees to comply with applicable Privacy Laws.
perturbation.changed_text: (f) Vendor agrees to comply with confidentiality guidelines.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the explicit requirement to comply with applicable 'Privacy Laws' with a vague 'confidentiality guidelines' undermines the legal enforceability of the clause and creates ambiguity. This directly contradicts the intention of the original clause, which aimed to ensure compliance with specific, legally defined privacy obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) “Confidential Information” a. means nonpublic information that IBC designates as being confidential. b. includes, without limitation, information in tangible or intangible form relating to the HCAI System. c. as between IBC and Vendor, shall also be deemed to include any Claim Request.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly required compliance with 'applicable Privacy Laws' when dealing with Personal Information. The changed text replaces this with a vague 'confidentiality guidelines.' The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly but does not address the specific legal obligations regarding personal information under Privacy Laws. Therefore, the change removes a key requirement related to privacy laws, creating a conflict because the original intent to comply with these laws is undermined, leading to a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (j) Vendor shall ensure that in its license agreement for the PMS Software: a. It includes protections that protec t IBC and its licensors and subcontractors from any liability to Vendor’s licensees in respect of such licensee’s use of any porti on of the PMS Software that is developed in reliance upon the Confidential Information; b. It includes intellectual property protections for the C onfidential Information that are no less stringent that those applicable in respect of the PMS Software but in any event prohibit any disassembly or reverse engineering of any component of the PMS Software that incorporates the Confidential Information (provided that a prohibition on reverse engineering of the entire PMS Software shall be sufficient to satisfy the requirements of this provision). c. It includes a copyright notice sufficient to protect IBC copyright in any redistributed software code. d. It contains a prohibition against any further distribu tion by such licensee of any component of the PMS Software that incorporates the Confidential Information. e. It includes a prohibition against the use of any portion of the PMS Software that is developed in reliance upon the Confidential Information for the provision of services relating to practice management to third parties. f. It otherwise complies with all of the requirements contained in an applicable EULA provided by or identified by IBC.
perturbation.changed_text: (j) Vendor shall ensure that in its license agreement for the PMS Software: a. It includes basic protections for IBC; b. It includes intellectual property protections; c. It includes a copyright notice; d. It contains a distribution prohibition; e. It includes a prohibition against use for practice management services; f. It otherwise complies with IBC requirements.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the detailed requirements for the Vendor's license agreement with generic statements significantly weakens the protection of IBC's Confidential Information and intellectual property. This weakens the enforcement of those protections and creates uncertainty around the specific obligations of the Vendor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) All Confidential Information, and all associated inte llectual property rights therein, are and shall remain the exclusive property of IBC and its licensors . By disclosing Confidential Information to Vendor, IBC does not grant any express or implied right to Vendor to or under any pate nts, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secr et information except as otherwise provid ed herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies detailed requirements for the Vendor's license agreement, including protections for IBC's Confidential Information and intellectual property. The changed text replaces these detailed requirements with generic statements. The contradicted text states that all Confidential Information and associated intellectual property rights remain the exclusive property of IBC and its licensors. The changed text creates a contradiction because the generic statements weaken the specific protections previously in place, making it less clear how IBC's property rights will be enforced and potentially undermining IBC's exclusive property rights, thus triggering a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor agrees to defend any and all Claims that may be brought or alleged agains t IBC and/or its resp ective directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors, and indemnify and ho ld harmless IBC and its respectiv e directors, officers, employe es, agents and contractors, from and against any and all Claims or damages that may arise, by reason of any breach of this Agreement by Vendor, its personnel or contractors or from Ve ndor’s use of the Confidenti al Information including any deficiency in or other issue relating to th e use or distribution of that portion of th e PMS System that is developed using the - 4 - PRACTICE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM INTEGRATION TOOLKIT NON-DISCLOSURE AND PERMITTED USE AGREEMENT Confidential Information. For any Claims that IBC chooses to defend, the Vendor sh all, to the extent requested by IBC, participate in and co-operate with and assist IBC in such defence a nd any related settlement negotiations.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor agrees to cooperate with IBC in the event of a claim. For any Claims that IBC chooses to defend, the Vendor shall participate and assist IBC in such defence and any related settlement negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specific indemnity and defense obligations drastically reduces Vendor's responsibility for claims arising from a breach of the agreement or use of Confidential Information. This omission directly contradicts the implied expectation of risk allocation inherent in a non-disclosure and permitted use agreement. Now, it is unclear on what terms the vendor agrees to cooperate in the event of a claim.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) IBC has or expects to enter into service agreements dire ctly with Providers that will govern such Provider’s access to and use of the HCAI System. However, IBC does not guarantee availability or ope ration of the HCAI System to Vendor. Without prejudice to any right an individual Provider may have against IBC, IBC shall have no liability to Vendor in respect of any failure or problem in the operation of the HCAI System, including any communication proble ms between Providers and the HCAI System. IN NO EVENT WILL THE MEASURE OF DAMAGES PAYABLE BY EITHER PARTY INCLUDE, NOR WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR, ANY CONSEQUE NTIAL, INDIRECT, INCI DENTAL, EXEMPLARY OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATI ON, DAMAGES DUE TO BUSINESS INTERRUPTION OR LOST PROFITS, SAVINGS, COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE OR GOODWILL) ARISING FROM OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION AND/ OR THE OPERATION OF THE HCAI SYSTEM, REGARDLESS OF THE TYPE OF CLAIM, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT (INCLUDING FUNDAMENTAL BREACH OR FAILURE OF THE ESSENTIAL PURPOSE OF THIS AGREEMENT OR ANY REMEDY CONTAINED HEREIN), NEGLIGENCE, STRICT LIABILIT Y OR OTHER LEGAL OR EQUITABLE THEORY, WHETHER OR NOT FORESEEABLE, AND REGARDLESS OF THE CAUSE OF SUCH DAMAGES EVEN IF THE PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES IN ADVANCE. IBC’S LIABILITY FOR ANY MATTER RELATING TO OR ARISING FROM THIS AGREEMENT, THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION AND/OR THE OPERATION OF THE HCAI SYSTEM, WHETHER BASED UPON AN ACTION OR CLAIM IN CONTRACT, WARRANTY, EQUITY, TORT, NEGLIGEN CE, OR OTHERWISE, SHALL NOT EXCEED, IN THE AGGREGATE AND IN RESPECT OF ALL ACTIONS, CLAIMS AND DAMAGES , AN AMOUNT EQUAL TO FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Vendor agrees to defend and indemnify IBC against claims arising from a breach or use of Confidential Information. The changed text significantly reduces this obligation to merely cooperating with IBC in the event of a claim. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which limits IBC's liability and specifies that damages payable by either party will not include consequential, indirect, incidental, exemplary or punitive damages and also caps the total liability amount. The original agreement had a clause of indemnification in case of breach of contract, but the changed text contradicts this. This implies an altered risk allocation and creates a contradiction regarding liability, especially since the contradicted section limits IBC's liability.

perturbation.original_text: (h) IBC may terminate this Agreement with or without cause upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the Vendor. IBC will not exercise such discretion to terminate the Agre ement with a specific vendor except for commercially reasonable grounds. All sections of this Agreement relating to the rights and obligations of the parties concerning Confidential Informat ion disclosed during the term of the Agreement shall survive any such termination. Upon any termination of th is Agreement, Vendor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to, as promptly as is practical, remove any functionality incorporated in the PMS System that is based on the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: (h) IBC may terminate this Agreement with or without cause upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the Vendor. All sections of this Agreement shall terminate.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the survival clause and the requirement to remove functionality based on confidential information after termination directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of a non-disclosure agreement, which is to protect confidential information even after the agreement ends. It is creating an ambiguity around what exactly happens to the confidential information upon the agreement termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Vendor shall: (i) Not use any Confidential Information except as required for the Purpose. For further certainty, the Purpose expressly excludes the development of or provision by Vendor of a gateway or any web-hosted service that consolidates the exchange of Claim Information between Providers and IBC or any web-based or application service provider ba sed practice management system. (ii) Not disclose any Confidential Information to third par ties, except as expressly provided in Sections 2(b) and 2(c) of this Agreement; (iii) Take reasonable security precautions, no less than those precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasona ble care, to keep confidential the Confidential Information; (iv) Refrain from disclosing, reproducing, summarizing and/or distributing C onfidential Information except as expressly required for the Purpose, and onl y as otherwise provided hereunder; and (v) Refrain from reverse engineering, decompiling or disa ssembling any software code disclosed by IBC to Vendor under the terms of this Agreement, except as expressly permitted by applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that sections regarding confidential information survive termination. The changed text states that ALL sections terminate. This directly contradicts section (a) of the Contradicted Text, which outlines Vendor's obligations regarding Confidential Information, such as not using it except for the Purpose, not disclosing it to third parties, and taking reasonable security precautions. If all sections terminate as per the Changed Text, Vendor would no longer be bound by these obligations, creating a clear and direct contradiction, as per the provided 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.
